summary.body: Computer Engineering undergraduate at UFS with experience in full-stack development, test automation, CI/CD, and applied research. I build software with focus on maintainability, delivery quality, and measurable impact, combining product validation with engineering practices.

bullet_points: Built a React Native (Expo) and Spring Boot mobile MVP for pilot launch, delivering five core flows (authentication, campaign creation, donations, donation history, and voucher creation/recovery), and automated 63 end-to-end regression scenarios for these flows using Maestro for UI automation and WireMock for API mocking, improving release stability and troubleshooting efficiency.
Eliminated single-provider dependency in the payment flow by integrating multiple providers with failover, preventing outages that previously blocked all transactions for over 4 days.
Reduced deployment time by 75% (from 1 hour to 15 minutes), saving ~1.5 SWE/h per week across 2 weekly releases, by implementing CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Terraform-managed AWS infrastructure.

Built a plagiarism-detection benchmark dataset by scraping Codeforces problems and solutions via a custom Python pipeline with parsing and retry logic, addressing the lack of reliable validation data in the literature; assembled 500 solutions to distinct problems and generated obfuscated variants.

Implemented a Python-based Tree-sitter converter to generate reversible syntax-tree JSON representations for automated code comparison, achieving same-language plagiarism-detection F1-scores of 86.36% for Python and 68.63% for C using the dataset.

Won 2nd place at the ChipLabs Hackathon for building an LLM routing module for multiple AI APIs using LangChain and PostgreSQL as a vector database, reducing inference cost by routing prompts to the smallest adequate model instead of a fixed default.
Achieved 280th place out of 984 teams in the SBC 2025 Programming Marathon (first phase) by coaching the team through training and qualification.
